understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the KA59 puzzle game by solving each level, where a level is won only when every goal tile marked '+' is simultaneously occupied by a selectable piece (and then progressing until the final WIN for the whole run).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn controls the currently selected piece (identified by player.id and player.position) and movement actions attempt to shift it by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable object (e.g., a block) is directly adjacent in that direction and space allows, it can be pushed along with the piece; otherwise obstacles like walls/boundaries can prevent or alter movement depending on their specific behavior. Levels can contain multiple selectables; using CLICK with an exact on-screen coordinate switches which piece is controlled. The movement.*_blocked flags are only heuristic hints about adjacent objects and do not guarantee that a move will fail.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the KA59 puzzle game by solving each level, where a level is won only when every goal tile marked '+' is simultaneously occupied by a selectable piece (and then progressing until the final WIN for the whole run).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn controls the currently selected piece (identified by player.id and player.position) and movement actions attempt to shift it by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable object (e.g., a block) is directly adjacent in that direction and space allows, it can be pushed along with the piece; otherwise obstacles like walls/boundaries can prevent or alter movement depending on their specific behavior. Levels can contain multiple selectables; using CLICK with an exact on-screen coordinate switches which piece is controlled. The movement.*_blocked flags are only heuristic hints about adjacent objects and do not guarantee that a move will fail.
